title: saxifraga bronchialis subsp. cherlerioides
intro: Saxifraga bronchialis subsp. cherlerioides, also known as Saxifraga or Purple Saxifrage, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Saxifragaceae family, native to the mountainous regions of the world, including Europe and Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, which are attributed to its rich content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of the common cold. In herbal preparations, Saxifraga bronchialis subsp. cherlerioides is often used as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional folk remedies to treat various ailments, including skin conditions and digestive issues.
